summary: A rectangular courtyard-style structure with four tall corner towers and an elevated central walkway/roof connecting them. Main walls are layered horizontal bands; towers are vertical dark wood pillars with small glass windows and mushroom-colored flat canopies edged in stone slab. Ground around building is cleared dirt with a low stonebrick perimeter.

shape.overall_form: rectangular footprint with four symmetrically placed tall corner towers and a raised central spine/roof connecting front and back
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along central longitudinal axis; roughly symmetric front-to-back and left-to-right

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: foundation blocks / low perimeter / decorative edging

materials.name: brick
materials.role: uncertain - possibly nether brick or dark block for flat roof center bands

materials.name: glass
materials.role: small windows in towers and walls

materials.name: fence
materials.role: small vertical decorative posts between foundation stones

elements: four tall corner towers with overhanging flat canopies
central raised spine/roof with stepped layered bands
layered horizontal banding on walls (alternating wood and light/hard blocks)
small square windows arranged vertically on towers
glow blocks (glowstone) placed on top-center of canopy and central walkway for lighting
rebuild_hints: Place a rectangular base of dirt/courtyard; set stonebrick low blocks spaced evenly around perimeter as base decoration.
Construct four identical tall towers near each corner; use dark wood logs as vertical supports with wood planks between and small 1x1 glass windows in staggered vertical rows.
Create flat overhanging canopies for each tower: dark center layer (nether-brick or dark planks) topped and edged with stone slabs; put a glowstone at canopy center.
Build a raised central walkway/spine between front and back towers using stone slabs; add stepped layered roof bands over the main hall using alternating dark and stone slab layers.
Walls of the main hall are horizontal bands: lower wood planks, middle stonebrick/stone slab band, upper wood planks; include small windows and vertical stone slab pilasters at transitions.
uncertainties: Exact block type used for the dark flat roof bands: appears to be nether-brick or dark red/brown blocks (uncertain).
Precise interior layout and number of floors inside towers and main hall are not visible; floors_estimate is based on external window spacing.
Ground material outside the building appears as coarse dirt or terracotta but exact block type is uncertain.
Some decorative small posts between foundation stones might be stone slabs or fences; ambiguous at this distance.
